text:
This paper attempts to examine the importance of church unity from Psalm 133. The psalm is set in the backdrop of the Israelite journey to Jerusalem on pilgrimage and is used as a figure of ground where the pilgrims journey through hardship on their way to the presence of God. Their journey culminates in worship joy because they journeyed together. A lower critical approach and word study method is employed to examine the ancient text of the psalm with the view of bridging the gap between the ancient and the contemporary and to establish a clear connection between the Bible and our modern day world. It is shown that in the New Testament church unity transcends religious dogmatics and theological doctrines which tend to create many sects and divide the worship in the churches. Members of the church of Christ are therefore urged to gather together in the name of God. In the final analysis, this study examines Psalm 133 in relation to unity and love, which are sometimes reduced to mere social ethics in the church of God, while at the same time these are reduced to theological objectives that we must strive to achieve as we worship God.

**Keywords:** Psalm 133, Textual Analysis, Hermeneutics, Unity, Body of Christ
explanation: Coherent academic prose with domain-specific terms and a clear argument structure; however, it lacks citations and concrete data, which can signal human authorship but could also reflect AI-assisted writing.
